preamble: The shift from an alveolar fricative [s] to a velar stop [k] is an unusual but attested sound change. It typically arises in specific cluster environments (like [sk] → [k]) or in dissimilation contexts where an [s] is retracted and strengthened to a stop. It can also occur in borrowings or lexical accidents.
phoneticEffects: Retraction, Fortition, Deletion of Fricative Component

examples.note: The /sk/ cluster simplified to /k/ in some dialects, effectively reducing /s/ in the cluster

sources: Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
Hock, H. H. (1991). Principles of Historical Linguistics.
